comments.text: For the example $\lbrace 1,4,9,16,25\rbrace$ mentioned on the page, it says that thousands of terms are needed before periodicity appears. But under the stated Dickson rule, namely excluding sums $a_i+a_j$ with $i,j\le n$ so that $i=j$ is allowed, I seem to get a period of length only 224: The periodic part starts at $a_{87}=440$. For example, $a_{87},\ldots,a_{92}=[440,443,445,448,453,471]$, and 224 terms later, $a_{311},\ldots,a_{316}=[1616,1619,1621,1624,1629,1647]$, which is exactly the previous list plus 1176. In general, $a_{n+224}=a_n+1176$ for $n\ge87$.
